name: Tucson (United States)
description: A white field with the seal of the City of Tucson in the centre. The seal is a yin-yang-like circle: the yellow upper half depicts the 1949 Tucson skyline, and the purple/blue lower half depicts the Mission San Xavier del Bac. The text "CITY OF TUCSON" appears above the disc and "ARIZONA" below.
history: Designed by Mary Crowfoot for a 1949 city seal contest. The seal she designed was adopted as the official city seal and as part of the official city flag on 5 January 1953.

features.symbolism: plain white field bearing the seal of the City of Tucson

features.role: seal of the City of Tucson, designed by Mary Crowfoot in 1949
features.symbolism: a yin-yang-style disc in which the yellow upper half shows the Tucson skyline of 1949 against the desert sun and the purple/blue lower half shows the Mission San Xavier del Bac ("the White Dove of the Desert"); together the elements convey the city's twin identity as a modern Arizona city built on a long Spanish colonial and indigenous heritage
